commands.review_summary:
The PR captures stripped DeepSeek DSML blocks, parses `invoke`/`parameter` markup into synthetic `toolCall` blocks, wires that recovery into the OpenAI completions stream, and adds parser/filter tests.

PR surface: Source +98, Tests +78. Total +176 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Yes at source level: the linked issue has live CLI evidence, and current main only promotes native `choiceDelta.tool_calls` while the DeepSeek filter strips DSML text. I did not run live provider credentials in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Human review is needed because the contributor must supply live patched-runtime proof and maintainers should approve the corrected text-to-tool execution boundary; automation cannot provide the contributor's real environment proof.

target.title: fix(agents): recover tool calls from DeepSeek DSML text markup
target.body:
## Summary

DeepSeek V4 / Microsoft Foundry sometimes emits tool call intent as DSML XML-like markup in the assistant text stream instead of using native OpenAI `tool_calls`. The existing DeepSeek text filter strips this markup from visible output but discards the content entirely, causing tool invocations to silently fail. The user sees the DSML text instead of an executed tool call.

This PR extends the text filter to capture DSML content between open/close tokens. After the stream ends, captured blocks are parsed for `invoke`/`parameter` markup and injected as synthetic `toolCall` blocks into the assistant output. The parser handles both fullwidth (`｜`) and ASCII (`|`) pipe delimiters, multiple invocations per block, and mixed string/numeric parameter values.

**Files changed:**
- `src/agents/deepseek-text-filter.ts`: Added `recoveredToolCalls()` method and `parseDsmlToolCalls()` parser
- `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts`: Wire recovery after stream ends, before the `hasToolCalls` check
- `src/agents/deepseek-text-filter.test.ts`: 9 new tests for capture and parsing

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: DeepSeek V4 / Foundry tool turns that emitted DSML markup as plain assistant text now have those tool calls recovered and executed. Before: DSML markup was stripped from display but tool calls were lost. After: the filter captures the markup and promotes it to executable toolCall blocks.
Real environment tested: macOS, OpenClaw source tree at upstream/main (5dccba7), Node 24, pnpm test suite.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the full deepseek-text-filter test suite (17 tests) and the openai-transport-stream test suite (388 tests) to verify no regressions.
Evidence after fix: terminal output copied below.

**DSML tool call recovery tests (9 new, 17 total):**
```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/deepseek-text-filter.test.ts
 ✓ |unit-fast| ../../src/agents/deepseek-text-filter.test.ts (17 tests) 3ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  17 passed (17)
```

**Transport stream tests (388 pass, no regressions):**
```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts
 ✓ |agents-core| ../../src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts (194 tests) 175ms
 ✓ |agents-support| ../../src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts (194 tests) 149ms
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  388 passed (388)
```

**Type check clean:**
```console
$ pnpm tsgo:core
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental
(zero errors)
```

**Parser coverage from tests:**
- Single invoke with string parameter (fullwidth bars)
- Numeric parameter values parsed as numbers
- ASCII pipe delimiters
- Non-invoke DSML content returns empty
- Invoke with no parameters
- Streamed chunks across multiple push() calls
- Multiple tool calls in single block
- No false positives on normal text

Observed result after fix: The `parseDsmlToolCalls()` function correctly extracts tool name and arguments from the DSML XML-like markup. The `recoveredToolCalls()` method on the filter returns structured `DsmlToolCall[]` objects. In the stream processor, these are injected as synthetic `toolCall` blocks with generated IDs, allowing the agent to execute them normally.
What was not tested: Live DeepSeek V4 / Foundry inference was not tested (requires provider credentials and a model that intermittently emits DSML text). The recovery is tested via unit tests that simulate the exact DSML markup format observed in issue #85918.
